title: Operators — Descriptors, Metaclass Operators, and Expression Trees
key_insight: At the expert level, operator overloading enables domain-specific languages (DSLs) embedded in Python. SQLAlchemy, Pandas query expressions, and PyTorch's autograd all use operator overloading to build expression trees — the real power of the Python data model.
common_mistakes: Not handling `obj is None` in `__get__` — when a descriptor is accessed on the class (not an instance), obj is None; failing to check this breaks class-level introspection
Building expression trees with mutable state — expression nodes should be immutable (frozen dataclasses); mutable nodes lead to subtle aliasing bugs
mini_exercise: Design a `Tensor` class where `t[0, :, 2]` returns a lazy `SliceExpr` object that, when `.eval()` is called, performs the actual indexing. This is the pattern used by JAX and Dask for lazy evaluation. What dunder methods do you need?
